prompt: I need to add a streak tracking feature to my Flutter app called ThinkDaily. Write the full stack: a StreakRepository (abstract interface + implementation), a Riverpod provider that reads from it, and a StreakWidget that displays the current streak count with loading and error states. The app uses flutter_riverpod with code generation (@riverpod annotation), GoRouter for navigation, and freezed for models. The repository implementation should return hardcoded mock data for now (no real backend). Save all output Dart files to the outputs/ directory.

assertions.text: Provider is defined using @riverpod annotation (code generation pattern), not manual Provider() or StateNotifierProvider()
Widget class extends ConsumerWidget (not StatefulWidget or plain StatelessWidget)
AsyncValue.when() is used to handle all three states: data, loading, and error
No setState() calls appear anywhere in the output code
An abstract StreakRepository interface class is defined, separate from the implementation
A concrete StreakRepositoryImpl class implements the abstract interface
